mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and equity multiple compression that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing construction and stable employment expansion confirms robust consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample rolling windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during supply chain normalization phases reduces input cost volatility and improves corporate margins, generating short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero correlation with 5-day SPX returns during identified recovery periods, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation expectations force anticipated monetary tightening, increasing the equity risk premium and discount rates, which mechanically compresses valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation readings do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales revenue coupled with accelerating real GDP growth confirms broad economic expansion, sustaining corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Combined signal produces negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-signal periods are not statistically distinct from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll growth reflects underlying economic strength and corporate hiring demand, providing steady upward pressure on equity prices over weekly horizons regardless of broader cycle phase.
failure_condition: PAYEMS trend shows no predictive power for 5-day SPX direction over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1 million units annually signal strong construction demand and consumer confidence, boosting cyclicals and overall equity returns during housing-led economic recoveries.
failure_condition: High housing starts fail to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns during rate-hike cycles, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.
